specific_mechanism: Programmed -1 ribosomal frameshifting: a heptameric slippery sequence followed by a downstream RNA pseudoknot causes the ribosome to deliberately shift its reading frame by -1 nucleotide at a specific position. The same mRNA span is then translated under the new frame, producing a different protein sequence. Stimulated by the pseudoknot's mechanical resistance to ribosome translocation. Used by HIV gag-pol, SARS-CoV-2, and many other viruses.
llm_application: Deliberate frameshifting in LLM context interpretation: a small structural cue (a 'slippery' token + a downstream structural marker) tells the model to re-read the same context span under a different interpretive frame, producing distinct downstream behavior. Distinct from role/persona prompting by being a token-local frame-shift rather than a persona swap. Distinct from chain-of-thought rewriting by re-using the same tokens under a new frame.
novelty_claim: Context-Aware Model Switching (2602.22261) switches BETWEEN MODELS based on complexity, not switching FRAMES on same model. ReFoCUS (2506.01274) does frame-level policy optimization for video-LMMs. Multi-Agent Reflexion (2512.20845) uses multiple agents. The 'same content, two interpretive frames driven by a structural cue' framing isn't directly imported, though role-prompting and persona-switching are well known broader approaches that approximate the effect.
